textPlain: I also like that it runs on bare metal. I wonder is it builds and runs on macOS? (I am on a mobile device and can’t check it myself.)Off topic, but I love the design of the linked web site.

This looks nice and very interesting that this runs on bare metal. Is more documentation / tutorials available anywhere?EDIT: Found the documentation:
https://scheme.fail/manual/loko.html
Nice achievement, building on the foregone dreams of Lisp powered workstations.We need more efforts like these and less yet another UNIX clone in C.

After skimming the docs and the site, I suspect this is x86-only for now. No mention of ARM or other processor architectures and the listed hardware support suggests a PC hardware target. You could probably cross compile on an Apple silicon Mac and run under emulation (e.g., QEMU).
